automotive-ev-battery.title: Ekoenergetyka introduces new product brand: Axon Charger
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Polish charging infrastructure manufacturer Ekoenergetyka has announced the launch of its new product brand, Axon Charger. According to the company, the new brand will not replace it but will serve as the 'product platform' and modern face of its charging business.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Sri Lanka urges daytime EV charging to ease grid pressure
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Sri Lanka is calling on EV owners to avoid charging at night, citing a peak in demand. Authorities aim to shift charging to daytime hours to better utilise solar power and reduce reliance on coal and diesel generation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Santa Monica breaks ground on $56m zero-emission bus and charging project
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Santa Monica, California, has launched a $56 million programme to expand its battery-electric bus fleet and charging infrastructure. Backed by state funding, the project supports up to 195 electric buses and marks a key step towards full fleet electrification by 2032.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EU: German government calls for further easing of CO₂ regulations
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The German government is seeking to ease the burden on motorists, including at the fuel pump. At EU level, however, the CDU/CSU and SPD also plan to advocate further concessions for the automotive industry that go beyond the European Commission’s proposals.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla Full Self-Driving Europe — Initial Impressions Review
automotive-ev-battery.summary:
On April 10th, quite literally at the 11th hour before Tesla’s self-imposed deadline, the Dutch road authority (the RDW) posted a press release that they have finally granted Tesla approval for Full Self Driving in the Netherlands. However, no one knew when the update would start rolling out. The picture   ... [continued]
The post Tesla Full Self-Driving Europe — Initial Impressions Review appeared first on CleanTechnica.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Is XPENG Building A Better “Tesla”?
automotive-ev-battery.summary:
Last week, reports indicated that Tesla was working on a smaller, more affordable model again. Even though it is reportedly in the initial stages of development, people got excited at the prospect of a more affordable Tesla. The share price was up for the week. Also last week, China’s Ministry   ... [continued]
The post Is XPENG Building A Better “Tesla”? appeared first on CleanTechnica.

automotive-ev-battery.title: A Startup Enters The Heat Pump Water Heater Space Focused On A Smart Water Heater
automotive-ev-battery.summary:
There are a lot of heat pump water heaters (HPWH) that have come to market recently. At the beginning of this decade, only a handful of manufacturers were making this hyper-efficient water heating product, and now we count 13 different brands of just tanked water heaters with more brands and   ... [continued]
The post A Startup Enters The Heat Pump Water Heater Space Focused On A Smart Water Heater appeared first on CleanTechnica.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Cleaning Up Lake Erie Begins With A Network Of Digital Sensors
automotive-ev-battery.summary:
Lake Erie is the shallowest of the Great Lakes and the most polluted. Now digital technology will help restore it to health. 
The post Cleaning Up Lake Erie Begins With A Network Of Digital Sensors appeared first on CleanTechnica.

automotive-ev-battery.title: France Moved First, But Markets Everywhere Are Signaling Electrification
automotive-ev-battery.summary:
France has made the first national push for increased electrification tied to the Strait of Hormuz-related energy crisis. Reuters reported that Paris will raise annual state support for electrification from €5.5 billion to €10 billion through 2030, while Le Monde summarized the package as including a ban on gas heating   ... [continued]
The post France Moved First, But Markets Everywhere Are Signaling Electrification appeared first on CleanTechnica.

automotive-ev-battery.title: What Does It Take To Save US Forests From Logging? A Justice Conservation Fund
automotive-ev-battery.summary:
The Puritan settlers of New England, steeped in the Old Testament biblical worldview, believed it was their God-ordained destiny to transform the dismal American wilderness into an earthly paradise. Seventeenth century Puritan writing is full of the idea of wild country as the environment of evil, and so they cut   ... [continued]
The post What Does It Take To Save US Forests From Logging? A Justice Conservation Fund appeared first on CleanTechnica.

finance-markets.summary: Final prewar cargoes to leave Gulf set to unload in coming days, escalating a global rush to secure barrels

finance-markets.summary: Pezeshkian says discussions can only happen within framework of ‘international law’

finance-markets.title: Mediators pursue Iran-US deal in back-channel diplomacy
finance-markets.summary: Trump says Iran representatives have ‘called’ and want a deal ‘very badly’

finance-markets.title: Starmer accused of ‘corrosive complacency’ on UK defence by former Nato chief
finance-markets.summary: Lord George Robertson, author of British military review, says premier is not willing to make the necessary investment

finance-markets.title: The end of the Viktor Orbán era
finance-markets.summary: Populist nationalism can be beaten at the ballot box, even if it can endure a long time

finance-markets.title: Trump deletes ‘Jesus’ post after backlash from allies
finance-markets.summary: US president circulated image of himself as Christ-like figure shortly after lashing out at Pope Leo

finance-markets.title: Trump nominates Pimco executive to lead US Treasury’s international affairs
finance-markets.summary: Bond giant’s head of asset allocation tapped to conduct negotiations with organisations such as the G7

finance-markets.title: Reeves tells Gulf expats: come to ‘safe harbour’ Britain
finance-markets.summary: UK chancellor is set to review tax rules that hit mobile professionals who are paid by foreign companies

finance-markets.title: Bain & Co vulnerability exposed by hacker a month after McKinsey
finance-markets.summary: CodeWall says it gained access to consultant’s Pyxis platform using a username and password from public web code

finance-markets.title: CATL Is Said to Consider Up to $5 Billion Share Sale in HK
finance-markets.summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. is considering a share sale to raise as much as $5 billion in Hong Kong after rallying strongly since its May listing in the city, according to people familiar with the matter.

science-space.title: NASA Invites Media to Rollout Event for Artemis III Moon Rocket Stage
science-space.summary: NASA will roll the largest section of the agency’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket, which will launch the second crewed Artemis mission, out of the agency’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on Monday, April 20. What’s called the top four-fifths of the SLS core stage – the section containing the liquid hydrogen tank, liquid […]

science-space.title: NASA Announces 32nd Annual Human Exploration Rover Challenge Winners
science-space.summary: NASA’s 32nd annual Human Exploration Rover Challenge, one of the agency’s longest-standing student challenges, culminated April 10-11 with its final excursion event at the U.S. Space & Rocket Center near NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center in Huntsville, Alabama. Spanning nine months, the challenge tasks student teams from around the world to design, build, and test […]

science-space.title: NASA Awards Data Engineering, Informatics Support Contract
science-space.summary: NASA has selected Development Seed of Washington to provide research and development services to the Office of Data Science and Informatics (ODSI) at the agency’s Marshall Space Flight Center in Huntsville, Alabama. The award is a performance-based, indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract with a maximum potential value of $76 million. A phase-in period begins on May 15, 2026, […]

science-space.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4852–4858: When Data Take Their Time…
science-space.summary: Written by Susanne P. Schwenzer, Professor of Planetary Mineralogy at The Open University, UK Earth planning date: Friday, April 3, 2026 I was the geology science team lead on Monday for planning Sols 4852-4853, when our data did not arrive on time for planning. Thus, we got creative as a team thinking what we could […]

science-space.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4845-4851: Bye-Bye Boxwork, Bye-Bye
science-space.summary: Written by Lucy Thompson, APXS Strategic Planner and Planetary Geologist at the University of New Brunswick, Canada Earth planning date: Friday, March 27, 2026 Last weekend’s drive took us just over the southernmost contact of the boxwork terrain with the surrounding layered sulfate unit. This was our third time crossing this contact, providing an excellent […]

science-space.title: NASA to Host Artemis II Crew Postflight News Conference
science-space.summary: Fresh off their return to Earth, the Artemis II astronauts will hold a news conference at 2:30 p.m. EDT Thursday, April 16, at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston to discuss their historic mission around the Moon. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, along with CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, […]

science-space.title: Artemis II Astronauts Aboard USS John P. Murtha
science-space.summary: The first astronauts to travel to the Moon in more than half a century are back on Earth after a record-setting mission aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman (commander), Christina Koch (mission specialist), Victor Glover (pilot), and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen (mission specialist) pose for a group photo in the well […]

science-space.title: NASA Night-light Imagery Tracks US Energy Transition, Global Volatility
science-space.summary: New nighttime maps based on NASA satellite imagery are upending assumptions, revealing a world where artificial brightening and dimming have intensified over the past decade. The findings show intense flaring over major oil and gas fields in the United States, while factors such as rural electrification and energy conservation are changing how billions around the […]

science-space.title: Sci-fi stories never really end anymore, and that's a problem
science-space.summary: It just goes on and on, my friends.

science-space.title: NASA science faces 'very serious threat' from new White House budget, experts say
science-space.summary: "This is the least transparent NASA budget request I've ever seen — and I've literally looked through every single one since 1960."

world-news.title: Lebanon enters talks with Israel but with no cards to play
world-news.summary: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

world-news.title: BBC joins paramedics on duty in Lebanon after Israeli strikes
world-news.summary: BBC Middle East correspondent Hugo Bachega joins paramedics on duty in Nabatieh - a city that was once vibrant, but is now abandoned.

world-news.title: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
world-news.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

world-news.title: Hospital at centre of child HIV outbreak caught reusing syringes in undercover filming
world-news.summary: Footage shows staff injecting without gloves and reusing syringes, but the hospital boss refuses to acknowledge it is genuine.

world-news.title: Harry and Meghan arrive in Australia for four-day tour
world-news.summary: It is the couple's first visit to Australia since 2018 when they were working royals.

world-news.title: ‘I don’t know why they’re coming’: Australians on Harry and Meghan's visit
world-news.summary: The BBC spoke to people in Sydney to hear their thoughts on the Duke and Duchess of Sussex’s trip to Australia.

world-news.title: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
world-news.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

world-news.title: Spanish PM's wife charged with corruption after two-year probe
world-news.summary: Begoña Gómez is accused of using her relationship with Pedro Sánchez to advance her private career.

world-news.title: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
world-news.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

world-news.title: Leo becomes first Pope to visit Algeria at start of major Africa tour
world-news.summary: Leo XIV wants the world's attention on a continent vital to the Church's future, an aide says.

asia-pacific.title: Asia air travel faces turbulence as Iran war exposes jet fuel vulnerability
asia-pacific.summary:
Jet fuel shortages and surging prices are forcing flight cancellations across the Asia-Pacific, a squeeze that could intensify after the United States began controlling oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz following failed peace talks with Iran.
Tehran’s effective closure of the strait – a chokepoint for about 20 per cent of global oil supply – is hitting regional airlines, particularly in countries without strategic energy reserves, analysts said. Jet fuel reached nearly US$198 in the...

asia-pacific.title: Shenzhen’s new party boss has taken the wheel but can he deliver ahead of Apec?
asia-pacific.summary:
As the new Communist Party chief of China’s southern manufacturing hub Shenzhen, Jin Lei is facing his first major test as the city prepares to host the Apec leaders’ summit in November.
Beijing is seeking more cooperation with the bloc in areas like artificial intelligence, and the key regional forum is an opportunity to show the potential in tech-driven Shenzhen, where Jin took the helm last month.
In recent weeks, the 56-year-old economist and former Sichuan official has been focused on...

asia-pacific.title: Malaysian bust spotlights shadow fleet oil trade in Southeast Asia
asia-pacific.summary:
The bust of an illegal transfer of oil between tankers in Malaysian waters has returned the spotlight to the shadow fleet trade in fuel – much of it from sanctioned nations – in Southeast Asia, which continues despite the paralysis of legitimate shipping routes triggered by the Iran war.
Two tankers were intercepted in waters off Bagan Ajam, off the coast of Penang, on Saturday during an alleged illegal transfer of more than 700,000 litres of diesel worth an estimated 5.43 million ringgit...

asia-pacific.title: Brussels must resist ‘passive’ role in US-China trade war, EU chamber urges
asia-pacific.summary:
A leading European business association in China has urged Brussels to avoid becoming a “passive recipient” of US-China trade negotiations, as European firms scramble to navigate Beijing’s export controls.
In a report released on Tuesday, the European Union Chamber of Commerce in China asserted that the EU must take the lead in discussions affecting its interests, while urging Beijing to avoid a one-size-fits-all approach to export controls.
“We’re in a situation where Europe simply cannot...

asia-pacific.title: Chinese man, 32, suffers stomach pain, discovers thermometer swallowed as child still inside him
asia-pacific.summary:
A 32-year-old Chinese man who went to hospital suffering from stomach pain was shocked to discover that a mercury thermometer he had swallowed 20 years ago was still inside him.
The man, surnamed Wang, from Wenzhou in Zhejiang province, southeastern China, had sought treatment at the Longgang branch of The First Affiliated Hospital of Wenzhou Medical University.

A scan revealed a foreign object in his duodenum, which doctors highly suspected to be a mercury thermometer.
As the tip of the...

asia-pacific.title: Minister says Pakistan ‘committed’ to repay US$3 billion loan from UAE
asia-pacific.summary:
Pakistan is considering financing from both countries and banks as options to repay a US$3 billion loan from the United Arab Emirates and help maintain foreign exchange reserve levels amid soaring oil prices, Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb said.
The South Asian country this month failed to reach an agreement with the UAE to roll over the debt for the first time in seven years, adding pressure on external buffers at a time when the economy is getting hit by the fallout from the conflict in..

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong taxpayers face HK$28 billion Covid bad-loan burden from SMEs
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong taxpayers may have to shoulder nearly HK$28 billion (US$3.57 billion) in bad loans from a now-defunct, fully government-backed financing scheme designed to help smaller enterprises survive the Covid pandemic.
Official data released to legislators showed that of the 67,189 loan applications approved under the special scheme, 13,231 had defaulted by the end of February, involving a total sum of HK$27.8 billion.
This translates to a default rate of 19.3 per cent.
As guarantor, the...

asia-pacific.title: Japan’s bid to avert Trump tariffs draws fire over climate impact of US projects
asia-pacific.summary:
Japan’s effort to shield itself from President Donald Trump’s tariffs is facing a new backlash, with environmental groups warning that US projects tied to Tokyo’s investment pledge could generate greenhouse gases equal to around 20 per cent of Japan’s annual emissions.
The Japanese government agreed to invest 5.7 trillion yen (US$35.6 billion) in the three projects as part of the first round of spending on US infrastructure in return for Trump’s tariffs being suspended. In total, the bilateral..

asia-pacific.title: Minnesota investigates ICE arrest of Hmong American man as possible kidnapping
asia-pacific.summary:
A Minnesota county is investigating the arrest of a Hmong American man by federal officers that was captured on video as a potential case of kidnapping, burglary and false imprisonment, officials said on Monday.
Ramsey County Attorney John Choi and Sheriff Bob Fletcher said at a news conference they are pursuing information from the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) that they need for their investigation into the arrest of ChongLy “Scott” Thao, 56, on January 18. Ramsey County includes the..

asia-pacific.title: Swalwell to quit US Congress following sexual misconduct allegations
asia-pacific.summary:
US congressman Eric Swalwell said on Monday he will resign from the House of Representatives, bowing to pressure from both Democrats and Republicans to leave Congress or face expulsion following sexual misconduct allegations that upended his campaign for governor.
The California Democrat vowed ‌to fight the allegations but said he must also “take responsibility and ownership” of his mistakes.
“Expelling anyone in Congress without due process, within days of an allegation being made, is wrong,”..

cybersecurity.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
cybersecurity.summary: Adobe has released an emergency security update addressing a zero-day vulnerability in Acrobat and Reader that attackers have actively exploited for at least four months using maliciously crafted PDF files. This critical flaw poses immediate risk to millions of users relying on Adobe products for document handling.

cybersecurity.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
cybersecurity.summary: U.S. and Indonesian authorities have dismantled a global phishing operation utilizing the W3LL toolkit, disrupting fraud attempts exceeding $20 million and arresting the alleged developer. The coordinated international enforcement action represents the first joint U.S.-Indonesia operation targeting a phishing kit developer.

cybersecurity.title: JanelaRAT Malware Targets Latin American Banks with 14,739 Attacks in Brazil in 2025
cybersecurity.summary: JanelaRAT, a modified variant of BX RAT malware, has launched over 14,000 attacks against Brazilian financial institutions and continues targeting Latin American banks and cryptocurrency exchanges. The threat steals financial data, logs keystrokes, captures screenshots, and monitors user input across targeted financial entities.

cybersecurity.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
cybersecurity.summary: The China-backed threat group APT41 is actively targeting major cloud providers including AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, and Alibaba Cloud using a sophisticated backdoor designed to evade detection and harvest cloud credentials. The campaign employs typosquatting techniques to obscure command-and-control communications.

cybersecurity.title: Critical Flaw in wolfSSL Library Enables Forged Certificate Use
cybersecurity.summary: A critical vulnerability in the wolfSSL SSL/TLS library allows attackers to forge certificates by exploiting improper verification of ECDSA signature algorithms and their sizes. This flaw impacts the security infrastructure of applications relying on wolfSSL for cryptographic operations.

cybersecurity.title: The Silent "Storm": New Infostealer Hijacks Sessions, Decrypts Server-Side
cybersecurity.summary: A new malware variant named "Storm" steals browser session data and enables attackers to perform server-side decryption, allowing session hijacking that bypasses password and multi-factor authentication protections. The threat bypasses traditional local decryption requirements, reducing detection opportunities.

cybersecurity.title: New Booking.com Data Breach Forces Reservation PIN Resets
cybersecurity.summary: Booking.com has confirmed unauthorized access to its systems exposing sensitive reservation and user data, prompting the company to force password resets for affected customer accounts. The incident highlights ongoing security challenges affecting major travel and hospitality platforms.

cybersecurity.title: European Gym Giant Basic-Fit Data Breach Affects 1 Million Members
cybersecurity.summary: Dutch fitness company Basic-Fit has disclosed a data breach compromising personal information of approximately one million customers. The incident underscores widespread vulnerability across non-technology sectors handling sensitive personal data.

cybersecurity.title: OpenAI Rotates macOS Certs After Axios Attack Hit Code-Signing Workflow
cybersecurity.summary: OpenAI has rotated potentially compromised macOS code-signing certificates following a supply chain attack in which a malicious Axios package was executed through a GitHub Actions workflow. The incident highlights emerging risks in software development pipelines and build automation systems.

cybersecurity.title: Over 20,000 Crypto Fraud Victims Identified in International Crackdown
cybersecurity.summary: An international law enforcement operation led by the U.K.'s National Crime Agency has identified more than 20,000 cryptocurrency fraud victims across Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United States. The coordinated effort represents a significant initiative to combat organized digital financial crime.

startups-vc.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch signals IPO readiness as AI agents fuel revenue surge
startups-vc.summary: Vercel, a developer tools and website hosting platform, is demonstrating strong growth driven by demand for AI-generated applications and agents, with its CEO indicating the company is preparing for an initial public offering. The company's ability to capitalize on the AI boom contrasts with many older startups struggling to adapt to the current market landscape.

startups-vc.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
startups-vc.summary: OpenAI has acquired Hiro, an artificial intelligence-powered personal finance startup, signaling the company's expansion into financial planning capabilities for ChatGPT. This acquisition demonstrates OpenAI's strategy to integrate specialized AI applications directly into its core platform.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: SiFive Leads With $400M For Custom Chip Designs As Aviation, Biotech And Defense Startups Also Raise Big
startups-vc.summary: SiFive secured a $400 million funding round for custom chip design services, leading a week of substantial capital raises across semiconductors, aerospace, and biotech sectors. The diverse mix of large rounds reflects investor confidence in infrastructure and specialized technology companies despite the absence of billion-dollar mega-rounds.

startups-vc.title: BillionToOne Goes Public — The Startup That Made Genetic Testing Universal
startups-vc.summary: BillionToOne, a Y Combinator-backed biotech company, completed its initial public offering and has become YC's fourth publicly traded biotech company. The company has achieved significant market penetration with its fetal genetic test now administered to approximately one in eleven babies born in the United States.

startups-vc.title: Meesho Goes Public
startups-vc.summary: Meesho, a YC S16 company, went public after building one of India's largest e-commerce platforms focused on empowering small merchants. The IPO marks a significant milestone for the founders and represents the maturation of an emerging market e-commerce business.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to Groww
startups-vc.summary: Groww, India's leading digital investing platform, completed its initial public offering, becoming Y Combinator's first company to achieve public listing status in India. The platform serves tens of millions of users investing in stocks, mutual funds, and exchange-traded funds.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
startups-vc.summary: EquipmentShare, a Y Combinator W15 graduate, completed its initial public offering after building an operating system for the construction industry across 373 locations nationwide. The company's IPO marks the successful scaling of a vertical-specific software solution from its Missouri origins to national deployment.

startups-vc.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
startups-vc.summary: Nuclear fusion startups have collectively raised $7.1 billion in funding, with the majority concentrated among a select number of companies leading the sector. The analysis provides insight into investor appetite for fusion energy technology and capital concentration within the emerging clean energy industry.

startups-vc.title: Why No One Has Enough FDEs, Why CS Can't Fill The Gaps, And Why Agents Most Can't Deploy Themselves. Not Yet At Least.
startups-vc.summary: Companies scaling AI agent deployments face a critical shortage of forward-deployed engineers, which represents a structural challenge beyond simple hiring constraints. The article examines why this talent gap is fundamental to current AI agent implementation capabilities rather than merely a recruitment problem.

startups-vc.title: The Leading Public Software Companies Are Now Down -50% in the Last 6 Months
startups-vc.summary: The SaaStr Index of the top 25 public software companies has declined 50.5 percent over the past six months, representing a severe contraction in market capitalization among leading B2B software firms. The extent of this decline significantly exceeds typical market corrections and reflects broader challenges within the public software sector.

crypto-web3.title: List of 'unsolvable' issues have shrunk, top White House crypto advisory says as momentum builds to pass legislation
crypto-web3.summary: President Donald Trump's top crypto advisor has indicated significant progress toward passing comprehensive cryptocurrency legislation, with previously contentious issues being resolved. The statement signals growing momentum for legislative action on crypto policy.

crypto-web3.title: White House crypto adviser Witt says other Clarity Act hurdles being cleared
crypto-web3.summary: White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt reported that a compromise on stablecoin yield is holding firm as the Senate advances the Clarity Act, despite continued warnings from banking sector representatives. The progress suggests the legislative path forward is becoming clearer despite industry opposition.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. lawmakers take another swing at crypto tax policy with revised bill
crypto-web3.summary: U.S. lawmakers have introduced a revised bill that would amend how the Internal Revenue Service approaches cryptocurrency taxation. The legislation represents ongoing efforts to establish clearer tax guidance for the digital asset sector.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. SEC says software allowing crypto wallet transactions not considered broker
crypto-web3.summary: The SEC clarified that software enabling securities transactions with individual wallets does not fall under broker-dealer regulations, providing regulatory clarity for wallet interface providers. This guidance reduces compliance barriers for certain cryptocurrency infrastructure services.

crypto-web3.title: SEC carves out path for some crypto interfaces to bypass broker registration
crypto-web3.summary: The SEC has established an exemption allowing certain cryptocurrency user interfaces to operate without broker-dealer registration, signaling regulatory flexibility for wallet and transaction platforms. This guidance represents a significant win for the crypto infrastructure sector.

crypto-web3.title: Ondo seeks SEC clearance for tokenized equities model on Ethereum
crypto-web3.summary: Ondo is pursuing SEC approval for a tokenized equities offering on Ethereum, with the SEC indicating openness to tokenization frameworks and encouraging direct regulatory engagement. The development suggests accelerating institutional acceptance of blockchain-based securities.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto exchange Kraken targeted in extortion attempt but says there was no breach and no client funds at risk
crypto-web3.summary: Kraken reported being targeted by extortion threats based on limited data access incidents affecting approximately 2,000 accounts, though the exchange confirmed no breach occurred and no customer funds were compromised. Kraken stated it will not negotiate with the criminals and is cooperating with law enforcement.

crypto-web3.title: Bankers rebuff White House claim that stablecoin yield doesn't threaten deposits
crypto-web3.summary: The American Bankers Association released a study contradicting White House economists' conclusions that stablecoin yields pose no threat to traditional bank deposits. The disagreement highlights ongoing tensions between the banking sector and cryptocurrency advocates over stablecoin regulation.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto wallet firm Exodus sues W3C and its CEO Garth Howat, seeking to compel $175M acquisition
crypto-web3.summary: Exodus has filed a lawsuit in Delaware Court of Chancery to compel CEO Garth Howat to comply with obligations under a November 2025 stock purchase agreement valued at $175 million. The legal action seeks to force completion of the acquisition.

crypto-web3.title: Former CFTC Chair Chris Giancarlo goes full-time as a tech and crypto advisor
crypto-web3.summary: Former Commodity Futures Trading Commission Chair Chris Giancarlo has transitioned to a full-time role as a technology and cryptocurrency advisor, having previously advised multiple firms including Polymarket and Paxos. His move reflects the growing demand for regulatory expertise in the crypto sector.

energy-climate.title: DOE proposes slashing non-defense spending on energy
energy-climate.summary: The White House has proposed significant reductions to the Department of Energy's non-defense budget, including cuts exceeding $15 billion in Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act funding designated for clean energy initiatives. This proposal could substantially impact federal support for renewable energy development and climate-related programs.

energy-climate.title: Vineyard Wind sues to force GE Renewables to complete work on 800-MW offshore project
energy-climate.summary: Vineyard Wind has initiated legal action against GE Vernova to compel completion of work on its 800-megawatt offshore wind project, citing a $853 million dispute over defective blade costs. The supplier has threatened to withdraw from the project over payment disagreements, creating uncertainty for one of the nation's major offshore wind developments.

energy-climate.title: Terra-Gen to pay $5.6M to settle CAISO market manipulation charges
energy-climate.summary: Renewable energy company Terra-Gen has agreed to a $5.6 million settlement with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission for failing to follow orders regarding battery electricity storage during high-price periods in California. The settlement addresses market manipulation violations in the CAISO electricity market.

energy-climate.title: Solar generation to rise 17% this summer: EIA
energy-climate.summary: The U.S. Energy Information Administration projects a 17 percent increase in solar generation during the summer months, underscoring renewable energy's expanding role in the nation's electricity supply. The forecast reflects continued growth in solar deployment and capacity additions across U.S. grid systems.

energy-climate.title: PJM proposes adding 14.9 GW with bilateral contracts, central procurement
energy-climate.summary: PJM Interconnection has proposed a two-phase plan to add 14.9 gigawatts of new generation capacity through a combination of bilateral contracts and central procurement mechanisms matching buyers such as data centers with new generation sellers. The proposal faces potential opposition from states and utilities concerned about affordability impacts.

energy-climate.title: St. Kitts and Nevis tenders major solar-storage project
energy-climate.summary: The electric utility of St. Kitts and Nevis has launched a tender for a 50-megawatt solar facility paired with 30.5 megawatt-hours of battery storage capacity. The project represents a significant renewable energy deployment for the Caribbean nation and reflects growing adoption of integrated solar-storage systems in island economies.

energy-climate.title: RWE installs CO2-reduced steel tower and recyclable blades at Thor
energy-climate.summary: RWE has installed an offshore wind turbine at the Thor wind farm utilizing a carbon-reduced steel tower and recyclable rotor blades, advancing sustainability practices in offshore wind manufacturing. The installation demonstrates the renewable energy industry's commitment to reducing embodied carbon and improving circular economy principles.

energy-climate.title: Geronimo Power begins operations at 117MW solar project in Ohio
energy-climate.summary: Geronimo Power has achieved commercial operations at its 117-megawatt Dodson Creek Solar Project in Highland County, Ohio, adding significant renewable generation capacity to the U.S. grid. The project represents continued expansion of utility-scale solar development in the Midwest.

energy-climate.title: Halocell Energy, Sofab Inks advance perovskite collaboration
energy-climate.summary: A collaboration between Australian Halocell Energy and U.S.-based Sofab Inks has demonstrated that perovskite solar modules incorporating Sofab's Tinfab electron transport layer maintain approximately 100 percent normalized efficiency after 1,300 hours of accelerated combined light and damp-heat testing. The results suggest promising durability for next-generation perovskite solar technology.

energy-climate.title: MyNu Energy launches mobile solar and battery energy storage system
energy-climate.summary: MyNu Energy has launched a mobile solar generation and battery storage system designed to reduce Middle Eastern dependence on diesel fuel amid regional energy security concerns and volatile fuel costs. The mobile system offers flexibility for distributed renewable energy deployment in regions facing fuel supply uncertainty.

health-biotech.title: Revolution Medicines touts 'unprecedented' data for pancreatic cancer pill
health-biotech.summary: Revolution Medicines announced Phase 2 trial results showing metastatic pancreatic cancer patients treated with the company's targeted pill achieved approximately twice the overall survival compared to standard chemotherapy. This represents a significant clinical advance for a disease with historically poor outcomes and positions the therapy as a potential new standard of care.

health-biotech.title: BioNTech's HER2 ADC succeeds in Phase 2 study, FDA filing planned
health-biotech.summary: BioNTech announced positive Phase 2 data for its HER2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate trastuzumab pamirtecan, with plans to pursue FDA approval. The therapy is positioned to compete with Roche's blockbuster Enhertu in HER2-positive cancers across multiple indications.

health-biotech.title: Allogene Therapeutics' CAR-T treatment eliminates residual cancer cells in B-cell lymphoma patients
health-biotech.summary: Allogene's off-the-shelf CAR-T therapy demonstrated efficacy in removing residual lymphoma cells in Phase 2 data, supporting the company's strategy to develop an accessible allogeneic cell therapy that could reduce relapse risk. The positive results strengthen Allogene's competitive position in the CAR-T market.

health-biotech.title: Maryland state affordability board sets its first price cap for a medicine
health-biotech.summary: Maryland's newly empowered state affordability board issued its inaugural price cap targeting the diabetes drug Jardiance, with implementation expected by January 2027. This landmark action represents the first state-level drug pricing intervention of its kind and could serve as a model for other states seeking cost containment.

health-biotech.title: FDA pushes drugmakers to report missing clinical trial results
health-biotech.summary: FDA analysis revealed that approximately 30 percent of clinical trials meeting mandatory reporting requirements had results that were not submitted by sponsors. The agency is intensifying enforcement efforts to ensure transparency and compliance with disclosure obligations for trial outcomes.

health-biotech.title: Access granted: CMS greenlights more than 150 participants for chronic care experiment
health-biotech.summary: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services approved over 150 digital health companies to participate in the ACCESS experiment, a novel chronic care management program featuring outcome-aligned reimbursement models. The initiative aims to accelerate adoption of technology-enabled care delivery with financial incentives tied to clinical results.

health-biotech.title: Spyre drug for inflammatory bowel disease shows promise in early study
health-biotech.summary: Spyre Therapeutics reported early-stage data for its IBD therapy demonstrating remission rates suggestive of a best-in-class profile relative to existing treatments like Takeda's Entyvio. The positive results indicate potential for a meaningful advancement in inflammatory bowel disease management.

health-biotech.title: Regeneron wades into radiopharma through $40M upfront deal with Telix
health-biotech.summary: Regeneron entered the radiopharmaceutical space through a 50/50 partnership with Telix Pharmaceuticals, with $40 million in upfront funding to develop therapies targeting solid tumors and diagnostic agents. The collaboration marks Regeneron's strategic expansion into the growing radiopharmaceutical sector.

health-biotech.title: White House policies are 'incredibly worrisome' for small biotechs, Rhythm CEO says
health-biotech.summary: The CEO of Rhythm Pharmaceuticals expressed concern that Trump administration policies including pharma tariffs and most-favored-nation pricing initiatives pose significant risks to small biotechnology companies with limited product portfolios. The comments highlight industry anxiety about regulatory and reimbursement pressures on smaller players.

health-biotech.title: Neomorph raises $100M for molecular glues; Spyre's ulcerative colitis data
health-biotech.summary: San Diego-based Neomorph announced a $100 million Series B funding round to advance its molecular glue technology platform for drug development. The substantial financing underscores continued investor confidence in targeted protein degradation approaches as a novel therapeutic modality.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI has acquired Hiro, an AI-powered personal finance startup, signaling the company's expansion into financial planning capabilities for ChatGPT. The acquisition demonstrates OpenAI's strategy to integrate specialized AI services directly into its core platform.

tech-ai.title: Read OpenAI's latest internal memo about beating the competition — including Anthropic
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI's chief revenue officer Denise Dresser outlined the company's strategic priorities in a memo emphasizing the need to lock in users and grow enterprise business while building competitive moats against rivals like Anthropic. The memo highlights OpenAI's concern about user switching costs in an increasingly competitive AI market.

tech-ai.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
tech-ai.summary: Stanford's latest AI Index reveals a widening gap between AI experts and the general public regarding AI capabilities and risks, with the public expressing rising anxiety about job displacement, healthcare impacts, and economic effects. The study underscores growing public concern about AI's societal implications as the technology becomes more prevalent.

tech-ai.title: Uber and Nuro begin testing premium robotaxi service in San Francisco
tech-ai.summary: Uber and autonomous vehicle company Nuro have launched testing of a premium robotaxi service in San Francisco, with Uber employees able to hail Lucid autonomous vehicles through the platform. The pilot marks a significant step in the commercialization of autonomous delivery and transportation technology.

tech-ai.title: Xbox Game Pass 'has become too expensive,' says Microsoft's new gaming chief in leaked memo
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft's new Xbox chief Asha Sharma acknowledged in an internal memo that Game Pass pricing has become prohibitively expensive for players and signaled forthcoming changes to the subscription model. The admission suggests Microsoft plans to restructure its gaming subscription strategy to improve value proposition and competitiveness.

tech-ai.title: FBI announces takedown of phishing operation that targeted thousands of victims
tech-ai.summary: Federal authorities have dismantled a significant phishing operation that utilized the W3LL phishing kit to compromise accounts and credentials of over 17,000 victims globally. The takedown involved cybercriminals stealing passwords and multi-factor authentication codes used in large-scale credential theft campaigns.

tech-ai.title: Booking.com confirms hackers accessed customers' data
tech-ai.summary: Booking.com has notified customers of a security breach in which hackers accessed personal information including names, email addresses, and phone numbers. The incident represents a significant data compromise affecting the travel giant's customer base.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft is developing a new AI agent framework designed for enterprise customers with enhanced security controls and reduced risk compared to the open-source OpenClaw agent. The development reflects industry efforts to create safer, more controlled autonomous agent architectures for business applications.

tech-ai.title: IBM pays $17M fine to end DOJ suit over DEI programs
tech-ai.summary: IBM has settled a U.S. Department of Justice lawsuit with a $17 million payment, resolving allegations that the company engaged in illegal diversity, equity, and inclusion practices. The settlement concludes federal litigation regarding IBM's employment and hiring policies.

tech-ai.title: AI influencers are 'everywhere' at Coachella
tech-ai.summary: Coachella attendees and social media followers have encountered numerous AI-generated influencers at the music festival, created using artificial intelligence tools to produce realistic digital personas. The trend highlights growing use of synthetic media and AI-generated content in entertainment and social media marketing.
